steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined portion: "temperature reductions were observed in the air immediately surrounding the mushrooms." Trace how this sentence relates to what comes before and after it in the passage.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It presents a tangential finding...that the experiment described later in the text was designed to explain.': The finding is not tangential — the next sentence says the reductions directly "inspired" the air-cooling device. And no experiment "described later" is trying to explain the reductions; the device is already built. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It provides empirical evidence to bolster the claim made earlier...that certain fungal species maintain a hypothermic state.': The earlier claim is about mushrooms achieving a hypothermic state through evaporative cooling. The underlined sentence is about air temperature around the mushrooms, a separate downstream effect. It does not directly bolster the hypothermic-state claim. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It identifies an unexpected observation that motivated the study of evaporative cooling...discussed earlier.': Gets the direction of causation backwards. The study of evaporative cooling in fungi is already established at the start; the underlined sentence is a finding from that study, not what motivated it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It establishes a finding central to the text's discussion of a practical application resulting from the team's study.': The next sentence says the reductions ("though slight") directly inspired the air-cooling device. The underlined finding is the bridge between the biological observation and the engineering application — it is central, not incidental, to the practical application. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
